my tip are just get yourself a ZERO international fee CREDIT card and some cash.
Grab for trasportation/food delivery/restaurant dine-in voucher, Ticketbox for show/concert/workshop ticket, Agoda for hotel/hostel/home stay. All of these app accept international visa also.
Most hotels/restaurants/coffee shops accept internation visa now, so save cash for none-visa small vendor or small transaction.

Actually, Apple Pay does work in Thailand, but you need to understand how payments work here.
Most shops use EDC terminals, so the key thing to look for is the Visa or Mastercard logo.
If you see those logos, it usually means contactless tap-to-pay is supported, including Apple Pay and Google Pay (Mobile NFC).
In shopping malls and major chains, card payments are widely accepted — places like KFC, McDonald’s, food courts in malls, gas stations, and many other stores.
At gas stations or regular shops with EDC machines, just say “credit card” or “debit card.”
Once the staff enters the amount, you can tap your phone or watch. Apple Pay works the same as a physical card.
⚠️Important tip:
Do NOT say “Apple Pay” or “Google Pay” in small or local shops.
Many staff are not familiar with those terms because Mobile NFC is still not heavily promoted in Thailand, and most shops don’t display Apple Pay / Google Pay logos.
If the machine supports tap-to-pay, it doesn’t matter whether you tap a physical card or Apple Pay — it’s the same system.
For convenience stores and small supermarkets, Mobile NFC often works with no minimum spend at places like
Lotus’s Go Fresh, Lawson 108, CJ More, Big C Mini, Tops Daily, Daily Queen, pharmacies, and many others.
However, always ask before paying:
•	Do you accept cards?
•	Is there a minimum spend?
•	Is there any surcharge?
Some shops may charge an extra around 2% per card/tap transaction, while others do not.
For 7-Eleven Thailand, there is usually a 200 THB minimum. Just say “credit card, tap”, and after the amount is entered, you can use Apple Pay.
In international brand stores like Supersports, Nike, JD Sports, support is clear, and you can say Apple Pay directly without any issue.
On the other hand, street food stalls and small local shops usually accept QR payments or cash only.
Even inside malls, some small booth-style shops still don’t accept cards.
Bottom line:
Apple Pay is usable in Thailand — you just need to look for Visa/Mastercard logos, say “credit/debit card,” and don’t rely on Apple Pay alone. 💳📱
